mechanism_narrative: Persistently high unemployment signals weak consumer demand and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as economic activity remains subdued. This effect is generally always present during periods of elevated joblessness.
evidence_refs: fact:Unemployment rate was historically high in mid-2009.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of periods when UNRATE is above 7.0%, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is maintained at historically low levels, it indicates an accommodative monetary policy that reduces borrowing costs and encourages investment, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a medium-term horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:The Federal Reserve had lowered the federal funds rate to near zero by late 2008.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of very low inflation and near-zero interest rates signals a deflationary trap or severe economic weakness, which can lead to negative S&P 500 forward returns as corporate profits are squeezed and demand remains stagnant, despite monetary easing.
evidence_refs: fact:Deflationary concerns were prevalent in 2009 amidst low rates.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery, an initial stabilization or slight increase in non-farm payrolls indicates the labor market is bottoming out, boosting investor confidence and driving short-term positive S&P 500 returns as a leading indicator of economic improvement.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 1-month change turns positive after 3 consecutive months of decline
evidence_refs: fact:Non-farm payrolls were severely contracting in early 2009.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances during a post-recession period, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A significant rebound in retail sales after a period of contraction indicates a recovery in consumer spending, which directly translates to improved corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns as economic activity picks up.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month average % change > 1.0% after 6 consecutive months of negative growth
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending was severely depressed during the 2008-2009 recession.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production reflects increasing manufacturing activity and overall economic expansion, which typically leads to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over a longer horizon as the economy strengthens.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production was contracting sharply in early 2009.
failure_condition: If the correlation between INDPRO 3-month growth and S&P 500 60-day returns is consistently negative over any 3-year rolling window, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp and unexpected drop in the Consumer Price Index signals accelerating disinflation or outright deflation, which erodes corporate pricing power and profit margins, leading to immediate negative S&P 500 returns as investors price in weaker earnings.
evidence_refs: fact:Disinflationary pressures were a significant concern in 2009.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rebound in housing starts and a positive shift in GDP growth indicate a broad-based recovery in demand, with housing often leading the way out of recession, which is highly supportive of S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month average % change turns positive AND GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized % change turns positive
evidence_refs: fact:The housing market collapse was central to the 2008 financial crisis, making its recovery a key signal.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances when both conditions are met, this hypothesis is falsified.
